description_long: This is a compact hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a textured, ribbed grip section, likely made of rubber or a similar material for enhanced hold. The handle displays a natural wood grain pattern, transitioning into a metallic axe head with a weathered, worn finish. The axe head is single-bladed, with a broad, slightly curved cutting edge and a pronounced beard, while the poll is flat and squared off. The blade is securely mounted to the handle, with no visible fasteners, suggesting a traditional wedged or friction-fit attachment. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or branding, and the materials and finish suggest it is built for practical use in urban or workshop environments. The object appears sturdy and functional, suitable for chopping or splitting tasks.
description: A compact hand axe with a curved wooden handle, ribbed grip, and a weathered single-blade metal head. The design is utilitarian and practical, with no visible branding or decoration.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full curved handle with ribbed grip and the broad, weathered axe blade.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the narrow blade edge and the alignment of the handle behind it.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's curvature and the flat poll of the axe head.
description_view_3: Top-down view emphasizing the ribbed grip and the thin profile of the blade edge.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with an oval cross-section, featuring a ribbed rubberized grip for secure handling.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad steel blade with a curved cutting edge, pronounced beard, and flat poll; no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged or friction-fit mounting with no visible fasteners.
